Macedonia · Heart of the Balkans

Sun on the water,
stone underfoot.

Lake Ohrid is older than almost any lake on Earth. Above it sit a thousand years of churches, Ottoman bazaars, mountain trails and food worth crossing a border for. Let us show you the real Macedonia.

Plan a trip See it on Instagram
1.4M yrs
Lake Ohrid is among the oldest lakes on the planet
▾

…

365
Churches said to ring the city of Ohrid — one a day
▾

…

10+
Peaks rising above 2,000 m for hikers and skiers
▾

…

Why Macedonia

A small country that keeps surprising you.

Snow-capped mountains, a turquoise lake, Roman ruins, Byzantine frescoes and Ottoman bridges — all within a couple of hours' drive of each other.

Landlocked in the southern Balkans, Macedonia packs an unusual amount into a compact space. Mornings can start with coffee in Skopje's Old Bazaar — the largest in the Balkans outside Istanbul — and end watching the sun drop behind the mountains across Lake Ohrid.

The country runs on hospitality. Expect long lunches, homemade rakija, peppers roasted over open fire for ajvar, and hosts genuinely glad you came. It is affordable, walkable and refreshingly uncrowded.

Featured

St. John at Kaneo

The little 13th-century church on a cliff over the lake is the postcard image of Macedonia — and an even better place to watch the sunset in person.

Stories

Lake Ohrid, one of Europe's oldest lakes

Up to 1.4 million years old and home to species found nowhere else on Earth.

A taste of Macedonia

Food & drink to try

From the national bean pot to Tikveš wine — flavours straight from the ground.

The long story

Layers of history you can still walk through.

Bronze-age observatories, a Slavic literary capital, Ottoman bazaars — Macedonia's past is unusually visible.

Where to go

Six places to build a trip around.

From the lake to the wine country — pick a base or string them together over a week.

Culture

Museums worth your time.

From Byzantine icons to a prehistoric village on stilts — Macedonia's museums tell the whole story.

✕

Getting here by plane

Macedonia has two international airports. Skopje International Airport (SKP) is the main hub with the most connections, while Ohrid “St. Paul the Apostle” Airport (OHD) is the smaller second airport, mostly with seasonal summer flights for visitors to Lake Ohrid. Below: country, city and airline – tap an airline to open its website. Most routes are seasonal and change often, so always check the airline for current schedules.
